tickets.title: Place Mobile Food Orders
tickets.description:
As a Student, I want to order food using my phone, so that I can avoid waiting in long cafeteria lines.

Acceptance Criteria:
- Students can browse the menu.
- Students can place orders from their phones.
- Orders are sent directly to the kitchen.
- Order confirmation is displayed.

tickets.title: Mark Menu Items As Sold Out
tickets.description:
As a Chef, I want to instantly mark menu items as sold out, so that students cannot continue ordering unavailable food.

Acceptance Criteria:
- Staff can mark items as sold out.
- Sold-out status is updated immediately.
- Students cannot order sold-out items.
- Sold-out items are clearly indicated.

tickets.title: Filter Menu By Allergies
tickets.description:
As a Student with food allergies, I want to filter menu items by allergens, so that I only see food that is safe for me.

Acceptance Criteria:
- Students can select allergen filters.
- Matching menu items are displayed.
- Filtered allergens are hidden from the menu.
- Filter preferences remain active during browsing.

tickets.title: Track Order Status
tickets.description:
As a Student, I want to track the status of my food order, so that I know when my meal is ready for pickup.

Acceptance Criteria:
- Students can view order status.
- Status updates in real time.
- Ready-for-pickup notifications are displayed.
- Order history is available.

analysis.ambiguity1: The requirement "We need to reduce the queue" is vague because it does not specify how much the queue should be reduced or how success will be measured. Different stakeholders may have different expectations of acceptable waiting times.
analysis.ambiguity2: The requirement "It gives me anxiety" is vague because anxiety is subjective and varies from person to person. The requirement does not define measurable behavior except the request to completely hide items containing peanuts.
analysis.conflictSolution: There is a conflict between the Marketing Department and students who may not want to receive frequent notifications. The solution is to allow students to opt in or opt out of promotional push notifications while still receiving important order-related notifications.
analysis.nfr1: Reliability: The kitchen display system must continue operating offline for at least 10 minutes and synchronize when the connection is restored.
analysis.nfr2: Performance: Order updates and sold-out status changes should appear immediately to users and kitchen staff.
analysis.nfr3: Usability: Students should be able to easily filter menu items based on allergy preferences.
